LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF TLES SCLAY'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE ORDER FILED JANUARY 23, 2017, DISMISSING THIS CASE AS TO ALL DEFENDANTS IS DENIED. THE MOTION FAILS TO MEET THE REQUIREMENTS FOR A MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION PER CCP 1008 AND, EVEN IF THE COURT IGNORED THOSE REQUIREMENTS, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW ANY FACTS OR CIRCUMSTANCES WARRANTING A DIFFERENT RULING ON DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO DISMISS THAT LED TO THE JANUARY 23, 2017 ORDER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG; DEPT. 302.

calendar.matters:
Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration
Off Calendar Mar-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
TLes SClay, in pro per, for plaintiffs;
Michael J. Garvin, of Sheuerman, Martini, Tabari, Zenere & Garvin, Tel. 408.288.9700, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. PLAINTIFF TLes SClay's Motion For Reconsideration of the order filed January 23, 2017, dismissing this case as to all defendants is denied. The motion fails to meet the requirements for a motion for reconsideration per CCP 1008 and, even if the court ignored those requirements, plaintiff has failed to show any facts or circumstances warranting a different ruling on defendants' motion to dismiss that led to the January 23, 2017 order. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-01-2017 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance by or on behalf of Plaintiff. Court ordered matter dismissed pursuant to CCP 583.210 and 583.250. Proceeding not reported. (610)

Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Action For Delay In Service Of Summons (C.C.P. 583.210)
Off Calendar Jan-23-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
T Leslie S Clay, aka Tles Sclay, (no contanct number provided) appears In Propria Persona as plaintiff

Attorney, Michael J. Garvin of the law offices of Sheuerman, Martini, Tabari, Zenere & Garvin (408.288.9700) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendant Marin General Hospital's motion to dismiss for delay in service of summons is granted. The Hospital is entitled to have this case dismissed per CCP 583.250 due to the failure to serve any of the defendants within three years after this case was filed. Plaintiff TLes. SClay's argument that her service of a CCP 364 notice extended the three year time period of CCP 583.210 lacks merit. Neither CCP 364 nor any case holds or suggests that service of a CCP 364 notice extends the mandatory period within which to serve a summons and complaint. Nor has Ms. SClay shown that anyone associated with the Hospital misled her, much less that she actually or justifiably relied on any asserted misrepresentation that is attributable to the Hospital. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
